text:
President Emmanuel Macron of France said late on Sunday that he had just spoken with his Iranian counterpart, Masoud Pezeshkian, and had called on him to end “attacks Iran is carrying out against countries in the region, whether directly or through proxies.” Macron also said he called for freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz, a vital economic route for huge amounts of oil and natural gas. Last week, Macron said that French warships could potentially be used to support security for commercial vessels in the strait, but he does not appear to have publicly responded to President Trump’s statement on Saturday urging France and other countries to send ships to the area.
Oil prices rose and stocks fell as trading got underway on Sunday in the futures market, which enables trading ahead of U.S. exchanges opening on Monday morning. Brent crude, the international oil benchmark, rose 0.7 percent to almost $104, while S&P 500 futures fell 0.2 percent.
title: Trump adds to speculation about Iranian leader’s condition.

citability.title: Trump adds to speculation about Iranian leader’s condition.
